ActionItem.description: A single extracted action the principal should take.

due_hint.description: Free-text due hint as written ('Friday', 'EOD'); not parsed.
due_hint.title: Due Hint

type.description: Discriminator: 'text' for a plain imperative action; 'link' when the action involves following a URL (url is then required and non-empty).
type.title: Type

url.description: The URL to follow for a 'link' action item; None for 'text'.
url.title: Url

ActionItem.title: ActionItem

DraftReply.description:
A drafted reply the agent proposes. Never sent without confirmation
(#1264) — this is a proposal, not an action.
DraftReply.title: DraftReply

EmailAddress.description:
A single email participant. ``name`` is optional (many headers carry a
bare address); ``email`` is required and must look like an address.
EmailAddress.title: EmailAddress

EmailCategory.description:
The five-bucket triage taxonomy (schema 2.0, #1615). Values MUST match
``triage_heuristics.ALL_CATEGORIES`` — the contract tests assert this.
EmailCategory.title: EmailCategory

EmailDraftRequest.description: Propose a reply and obtain a confirmation token for it.

provider.description: Optional provider binding ('google' or 'microsoft'). When set, the confirmation token is bound to this provider so the send routes to the correct mailbox even when multiple are connected.
provider.title: Provider

EmailDraftRequest.title: EmailDraftRequest

EmailDraftResponse.description: The proposed reply plus a single-use confirmation token bound to it.

confirmation_token.description: Echo this back to POST /v1/email/send to authorize sending exactly this payload. Single-use; bound to (to, subject, body).
confirmation_token.title: Confirmation Token

EmailDraftResponse.title: EmailDraftResponse

EmailMessage.description: One email message. Shared by the single-email and thread inputs.

from.description: Sender. Aliased to 'from' (a Python keyword) on the wire.

thread_id.description: Provider thread id this message belongs to.
thread_id.title: Thread Id

EmailMessage.title: EmailMessage

EmailPreScanRequest.description: Request envelope for an inbox pre-scan (#1778). Read-only.

max_messages.description: How many recent inbox messages to scan. Bounded so a caller can't request an unbounded mailbox sweep.
max_messages.title: Max Messages

schema_version.description: Contract version. Mismatch lets a consumer fail loudly.
schema_version.title: Schema Version

EmailPreScanRequest.title: EmailPreScanRequest

EmailPreScanResult.description:
The aggregate pre-scan envelope the ``EmailPreScanCard`` renders.

This is the exact payload the chat surface detects via
``kind == "email_pre_scan"`` — produced by the agent's
``pre_scan_inbox_impl`` (same classification path), not re-implemented here.

informational_count.description: Count of informational (FYI/PERSONAL) messages — not listed.
informational_count.title: Informational Count

kind.description: Discriminator the chat surface detects to render the card.
kind.title: Kind

preferences_applied.description: Session preferences that shaped this pre-scan.

suggested_archives.description: Promotional / low-priority messages suggested for archive (capped).
suggested_archives.title: Suggested Archives

suggested_drafts.description: Reserved for future LLM-driven draft generation; empty today.
suggested_drafts.title: Suggested Drafts

EmailPreScanResult.title: EmailPreScanResult

EmailSendRequest.description: Send a reply. Requires a valid confirmation token for this payload.

confirmation_token.description: Confirmation token from POST /v1/email/draft. A send without a valid token for this exact payload is rejected (403).
confirmation_token.title: Confirmation Token

provider.description: Optional provider ('google' or 'microsoft'), used ONLY as the fallback when the confirmation token carries no provider binding. A token's bound provider always wins; with two mailboxes connected and neither a binding nor this field set, the send is rejected as ambiguous (400).
provider.title: Provider

EmailSendRequest.title: EmailSendRequest

sent_id.description: Provider message id of the sent email.
sent_id.title: Sent Id

to.description: Recipients the message was sent to.
to.title: To

EmailTriageRequest.description: Top-level request envelope shared by REST (#1229) and MCP stdio (#1104).

context.description: Optional context (people/projects/tone/self-email) that biases categorization and summary. Absent → behavior unchanged.

schema_version.description: Contract version. Mismatch lets a consumer fail loudly.
schema_version.title: Schema Version

EmailTriageRequest.title: EmailTriageRequest

EmailTriageResponse.description: Top-level response envelope shared by REST (#1229) and MCP stdio (#1104).

request_kind.description: Which input shape produced this result.
request_kind.title: Request Kind

EmailTriageResponse.title: EmailTriageResponse

EmailTriageResult.description: The structured analysis of a single email or thread.

category.description: One of the five taxonomy buckets (schema 2.0).

draft.description: Proposed reply, or null when none is suggested.

message_id.description: Echoes the provider message-id from the request (SingleEmailInput.message or ThreadInput.thread_id). Null when the result was produced from a raw Gmail-API message (no contract message_id available).
message_id.title: Message Id

suggested_action.description: Suggested next action: reply (URGENT/NEEDS_RESPONSE), archive (PROMOTIONAL), or none (FYI/PERSONAL). Derived by precedence rule -- never required so existing consumers are unaffected.
suggested_action.title: Suggested Action

usage.description: LLM usage metrics (tokens + aggregate TPS) for this triage. Null on the heuristic-only path where no LLM call was made.

EmailTriageResult.title: EmailTriageResult

HealthResponse.description:
Liveness/readiness probe for the email surface.

Dependency-light by design: it never touches a live mailbox or the LLM, so a
host can confirm the router is mounted and serving before any connector or
model is configured.

status.description: Always 'ok' when the surface is serving.
status.title: Status

HealthResponse.title: HealthResponse

PreScanItem.description:
One surfaced inbox message in a pre-scan section.

``why`` carries the rationale for urgent/actionable rows; ``reason`` carries
it for suggested-archive rows. The frontend card reads ``reason ?? why``, so
exactly one is populated per row depending on the section.

reason.description: Rationale for a suggested-archive row (the heuristic reason).
reason.title: Reason

sender.description: Raw 'From' header of the message (display + address).
sender.title: Sender

thread_id.description: Provider thread id this message belongs to.
thread_id.title: Thread Id

why.description: Rationale for an urgent/actionable row (the heuristic reason).
why.title: Why

PreScanItem.title: PreScanItem

PreScanPreferencesApplied.description:
The session sender/category preferences that shaped this pre-scan.

Always present (with empty defaults) so the frontend schema can lock in now;
the stateless REST surface leaves these empty today, but the field carries a
stable shape for a future preference-aware caller.
PreScanPreferencesApplied.title: PreScanPreferencesApplied

PreScanTotals.description:
Pre-cap counts per bucket — the true totals before the per-section caps
are applied to the surfaced lists. Lets the card show "N surfaced" honestly.
PreScanTotals.title: PreScanTotals

SingleEmailInput.description: A single email to triage (AC3 — single-email path).

principal.description: The recipient the agent acts on behalf of — whose inbox this is. Distinct from per-message 'to': the principal is the account owner, not necessarily a recipient of every message in a thread.

SingleEmailInput.title: SingleEmailInput

ThreadInput.description: A full conversation thread to triage (AC3 — thread path).

messages.description: Every message in the thread, oldest-first. Non-empty.
messages.title: Messages

principal.description: The recipient the agent acts on behalf of — whose inbox this is. Distinct from per-message 'to': the principal is the account owner, not necessarily a recipient of every message in a thread.

thread_id.description: Provider thread id for the conversation.
thread_id.title: Thread Id

ThreadInput.title: ThreadInput

TriageContext.description:
Optional caller-supplied context that biases categorization/summary
(#1541). Absent → behavior is identical to today. Coexists with gaia
memory (#1114) without requiring it.

people.description: Important people whose mail should weigh higher.
people.title: People

projects.description: Active projects the principal cares about.
projects.title: Projects

self_email.description: The principal's own address, so the model knows who 'I' is.
self_email.title: Self Email

TriageContext.title: TriageContext

TriageUsage.description:
LLM usage metrics for a triage, aggregated across the classify +
summarize calls. Reuses the existing ``AgentResponse.stats`` measurement
(#1277/#1278) — no new measurement path. ``None`` on the heuristic-only
path where no LLM call is made.

completion_tokens.description: Sum of output (completion) tokens across the LLM calls.
completion_tokens.title: Completion Tokens

prompt_tokens.description: Sum of input tokens across the LLM calls.
prompt_tokens.title: Prompt Tokens

tokens_per_second.description: Aggregate decode throughput (total output tokens / total decode time).
tokens_per_second.title: Tokens Per Second

total_tokens.description: Sum of input + output tokens across the LLM calls.
total_tokens.title: Total Tokens

TriageUsage.title: TriageUsage

VersionResponse.description:
The two version numbers a host negotiates against.

``apiVersion`` is the frozen REST/contract version (a contract bump bumps it);
``agentVersion`` is the package build. Both come from
``gaia_agent_email.version`` so this endpoint and the freeze server's
``/version`` report identical values.
VersionResponse.title: VersionResponse

info.description: REST surface for the GAIA Email Triage agent (/v1/email/*). Cross-implementation contract for the npm client and native builds.
info.title: GAIA Email Agent API

post.description:
Propose a reply and mint a confirmation token bound to its payload.

The returned token must be echoed to :func:`send_email` to authorize
sending exactly this ``(to, subject, body)``. This is the explicit
user-confirmation step — the consuming app surfaces the draft to the
user, and only a user-approved send echoes the token back.

get.description:
Report that the email REST surface is mounted and serving.

Dependency-light: no live mailbox, no LLM. A host uses this for the sidecar
readiness handshake and for liveness checks once mounted on the product app.

post.description:
Pre-scan the connected inbox into the scannable triage card envelope.

Lists the ``max_messages`` most-recent inbox messages via the connected
mailbox backend and returns the aggregate pre-scan summary the Agent UI's
``EmailPreScanCard`` renders — top urgent / actionable rows, an
informational count, and suggested archives, each with a heuristic reason.
Read-only: nothing is archived, marked, or sent.

Classification reuses the agent's ``pre_scan_inbox_impl`` (the same
heuristic path the agent loop runs) — categories are not re-implemented
here. The backend is resolved by :func:`get_prescan_backend`, which fails
loudly on zero or ambiguous mailboxes.

Send a reply — gated on explicit confirmation (#1264).

The confirmation gate is enforced FIRST: a request without a valid,
payload-bound confirmation token is rejected with HTTP 403 before any
backend call (or even backend resolution). This mirrors the agent's
``TOOLS_REQUIRING_CONFIRMATION`` guard, translated to the API boundary,
and guarantees the gate fires regardless of backend health. Never
auto-confirms.

Triage a single email or a full thread.

Accepts the FROZEN #1262 ``EmailTriageRequest`` (a single-email or
thread payload, discriminated on ``kind``) and returns the structured
``EmailTriageResponse`` — category, spam/phishing signals, a plain-text
summary, extracted action items, and an optional draft-reply proposal.
No mail is read or sent; this analyzes only the payload in the request.

get.description:
Report the REST/contract version and the package build version.

Both values come from ``gaia_agent_email.version`` — the same constants the
freeze server's root ``/version`` reads — so the product surface and the
frozen binary can never disagree on what contract they speak.
